events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: Art by Kazui Ikenshi
events.description: Throughout the month of June, works by artist Kazui Ikenishi are on display at Bloor Gladstone Library in an exhibit. Kazui Ikenishi is an abstract painter born and raised in Japan, now based in Toronto, Canada. A self-taught artist, she began painting in September 2023 and quickly developed a passion for vibrant, expressive compositions. events.description: Participate in a library scavenger hunt and learn some cool facts. Answer all questions correctly for a chance to win a prize! Aimed at children ages 4 and up.  Drop in. Registration is not required. Larger groups are encouraged to call the branch in advance.

events.title: * WonderPhil Magic Show *
events.description: The amazing WonderPhil performs a kids & family magic show that's packed with laughs, audience participation and incredible tricks that also promotes the enjoyment of books and reading! This program runs for 60 minutes and features the full WonderPhil Magic Show as described, followed by a magic workshop. WonderPhil will teach some tricks that the kids can do at home! Drop-in. Children of all ages are welcome.

Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as he leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "real magicians" during this fast-paced, fun and amazing magic show! Register here or by calling 416-396-8939.

events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. It opens on Tuesday, May 19 following the holida

events.title: Month long Scavenger Hunt: Follow the rainbowsaurus trail!
events.description: Jones Branch would like to invite you on a fun and magical quest to find the legendary Rainbowsaurus and all 11 of his colourful friends! Once you found all 12 animals on your scavenger hunt sheet, you will earn a certificate! For school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in.

events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: Art by Kazui Ikenshi
events.description: Throughout the month of June, works by artist Kazui Ikenishi are on display at Bloor Gladstone Library in an exhibit. Kazui Ikenishi is an abstract painter born and raised in Japan, now based in Toronto, Canada. A self-taught artist, she began painting in September 2023 and quickly developed a passion for vibrant, expressive compositions. Working primarily with acrylic paint, she embraces bold colors and dynamic forms in her artwork. Her creative process is deeply intuitive, often

events.title: Soccer Scavenger Hunt
events.description: Fort York is full of soccer! Drop by the branch to check out our June Scavenger Hunt. Search the first floor for soccer balls and win a special 3D printed prize when you find them all!

events.title: Sunday Crafternoon
events.description: Join us for Sunday crafts. Different activity each week. Sundays 12-4:30 pm. Drop-in.

events.title: Sew Cool Kids:  Cross-Stitch
events.description: This fun and creative 4 week program introduces children aged 6 - 12 to the art of cross-stitching! Kids will learn the basic stitches, how to follow simple patterns, and how to use colorful threads to bring a design to life.  By the end of the program, each child will have completed a bright and cheerful stitched picture to proudly take home and display. Registering will register your child for all 4 sessions.

events.title: Wonder Workshop: Pollution Challenge
events.description: Do you ever think about the impact of one little person or one tiny thing on the planet? When one pollutant becomes many, the planet hurts. But when one person becomes many, positive change can happen. In this workshop, you will design and build your own creature or machine that will help keep our precious planet clean! Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book. For kids ages 6-12.

events.title: How to Write Non-fiction
events.description: Writing nonfiction can be a challenge but author Erin Silver will make it fun and easy! We will discuss what nonfiction writing is, how we can make it sound more like fiction, and how to organize and write engaging A+ nonfiction! Erin will share insider tips about her process, where her ideas come from, and will also read from her nonfiction book, Rush Hour: Navigating Our Global Traffic Jam .

events.description: The North York Central Library and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Please note, the tour will start and end at the North York Central Library, Room 1, and will be guided by the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff

events.title: "Jane and the Blue Willow Princess" with Author Catherine Little
events.description: "On a beautiful spring day, sisters Jane and Cassandra enjoy tea with Mother in the garden. Jane is in the throes of writing a story for Father’s birthday, but too many ideas swirl in her head, and she cannot get started. events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! For children ages 6-12. Parents are welcome to stay during the program, but please only register children. Informatio

The program concludes with a Comic Con showcase, where their work is shared with the community. For ages 9-12. Spaces are limited.  Registration required.

events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - The Stand
events.description: On a misty morning in the fall of 1985, a small group of Haida people blockaded a muddy dirt road on Lyell Island, demanding the government work with Indigenous people to find a way to protect the land and the future. In a riveting new feature documentary drawn from more than a hundred hours of archival footage and audio, award-winning director Christopher Auchter ( Now Is the Time ) recreates the critical moment when the Haida Nation’s resolute act of vision and conscience changed the world. events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen
events.description: Come join us for a radical retro rewind projected on the big screen. Whether you're a first-time viewer or long-time fan, these movies are sure to please. May 28: Star Trek (2009, PG) June 4: Crazy Stupid Love (2001, PG) June 11: The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014, 14A) June 18: Best in Show (2000, PG) June 25: The Big Lebowski (1998, 14A) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Before the Blue Jays: Professional Baseball in Toronto Prior to 1977
events.description: Harvey Sahker presents his book, " Before the Blue Jays " (Dundurn, 2026).​ Discover the fascinating history of professional baseball in Toronto before 1977, when the Blue Jays first took flight. Learn about the major events, venues, and people who trail-blazed the way to the arrival of Major League Baseball in Toronto.

events.title: The A(i), B, C’s of Artificial Intelligence
events.description: This week-long workshop series introduces teens to the four "must knows" of Artificial Intelligence. Through video clips, props, an original AI literacy song, discussions, and hands-on activities, participants will explore how AI works, how it’s used, and how to think critically about its impact. By the end of the program, attendees will be better equipped to navigate AI and social media with awareness and creativity. The workshop is led by Keith McDonald, founder and Chief Curator of Toron

events.title: Beginner Ukulele Workshop
events.description: Have fun learning a new instrument - the ukulele! This workshop will cover fundamentals of ukulele playing: the parts of a ukulele, tuning, strumming patterns, and some basic chords. By the end of this class, you'll have learned the skills you need to start playing your first songs on the ukulele! Beginner friendly, no previous experience is necessary. For ages 7 to 17. Please register, if you need to borrow a ukulele for this session.  If you have your own instrument, you may drop-in, no r

events.title: Beginner's Guide to Making Comics
events.description: Have you ever wanted to create your own comic, manga, or anime-inspired story? In this interactive workshop, teens will learn the basics of comic creation through hands-on activities, creative storytelling, and guided drawing exercises. Participants will explore comic and manga techniques including panels, page layouts, speech bubbles, sound effects, character design, and visual storytelling. They’ll also learn how to build engaging characters and create their own one-page comic or manga scene.

events.title: Youth Hub: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.description: This program is for kids and youth in grades 3-12. Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! events.description: Have an idea for a movie, but not sure how to write it? events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Saturday Knit & Crochet Circle
events.description: Want to learn how to knit or crochet with a fun group of people? Looking for help with your project? You can also share your projects with others, teach or learn a technique, and enjoy great conversation. All levels of expertise are welcome. Bring your own yarn, needles, and crochet hooks. All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: Youth Hub: Cricut Mugs
events.description: Turn a plain mug into a masterpiece! Join us for a hands-on workshop where you will learn to use Cricut machine to design, cut, and press your very own custom mug. events.title: LivingWorks SafeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks SafeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks SafeTALK certificat

events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by
